assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing Security Configuration section with Product Lifecycle, Version Streams, and Source Repositories unchanged
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 42-63 contain the full Security Configuration section with all three subsections (Product Lifecycle, Version Streams, Source Repositories) exactly matching the reference file. Product Lifecycle has all 5 fields (Product pages URL, Jira version prefix, Vulnerability issue type ID, Component label pattern, VEX Justification custom field), Version Streams has the 2.1.x stream row, and Source Repositories lists backend and frontend-ui with their URLs -- all identical to the original.

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md reports that Security Configuration is already fully configured or up to date

assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md indicates Security Configuration was preserved with no changes needed
assertion_results.evidence: changes-log.md line 23 shows: '| `## Security Configuration` | No change | Fully populated: Product Lifecycle (5 fields), Version Streams (1 stream), Source Repositories (2 repos) |'. Additionally line 10 states: 'No changes were applied. The existing configuration is preserved as-is.'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT offer the Security Configuration opt-in prompt — it detects the section already exists and is fully populated (idempotency skip)
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md Step 10 explicitly states 'Security Configuration exists with all required fields populated and no placeholder markers' with result 'Security Configuration is up to date.' No opt-in prompt is mentioned in any output file. changes-log.md marks Security Configuration as 'No change'. The skill detected the existing fully-populated section and skipped it.
